key_insights.insight: True success comes from rebelling for a purpose that resonates deeply with your values, not just for the sake of rebelling.
key_insights.quote: I always believe that I was rebelling for purpose.

key_insights.insight: Confidence is built incrementally through small wins that validate your abilities, creating a staircase towards larger achievements.
key_insights.quote: It's the confidence in the small stuff that actually builds the confidence towards the big stuff.

key_insights.insight: To find your 'why,' reflect on what you loved as a child, identify role models, and explore what captures your attention, allowing yourself to be pulled towards those interests.
key_insights.quote: To ask yourself, what did you love when you were younger, right? When did you light up? Who are your role models and inspirations?

key_insights.insight: Being 'mission obsessed' rather than 'product obsessed' allows for flexibility and adaptation in business, prioritizing solving the core problem over clinging to a specific product vision.
key_insights.quote: To be a mission obsessed, not product obsessed.

key_insights.insight: Failure should be viewed as a valuable source of data and feedback to improve and pivot, not as a reflection of inadequacy.
key_insights.quote: About failure being a data point, not an end point.

key_insights.insight: It's important to surround yourself with people who lift you up and believe in you and it's your choice who to surround yourself with.
key_insights.quote: You have to remember that the people you surround yourself with are your choice.

key_insights.insight: Having savings enables you to pursue your dreams with more security, allowing focus on the business without financial constraint.
key_insights.quote: I had three years to build this. I had three years before I ran out ofmy own cash.

key_insights.insight: Earning your stripes and doing good work, even in a job you don't love, builds a reputation and creates opportunities when you decide to pursue your passions.
key_insights.quote: It's always important to do good work because if he had ever heard oh pile is not good imean he knew that i was a good worker no matter what i did even though i didn't like once againlove my job i always did good good work and i know that I, that reputation followed me and he knew that.

actionable_advice.advice: Identify your 'why' by reflecting on childhood passions, role models, and current curiosities.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate time for introspection. Ask yourself what made you 'light up' as a child, who inspires you and what draws your attention.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing a career crossroads, evaluate the 'trade-off' – what you are sacrificing for your current path.
actionable_advice.how_to: List your values and compare them to your daily actions and sacrifices you have to make to earn a living.

actionable_advice.advice: When starting a business, become 'mission-obsessed' instead of product-obsessed.
actionable_advice.how_to: Begin by thoroughly researching the core problem you're aiming to solve, rather than being fixed on a particular product or solution.

actionable_advice.advice: Set goals not just professionally but also in your personal life.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set out time to create a list of what you want to achieve in the next 6 months or year relating to health, relationships, family and fun, as well as your career.

memorable_stories: The story of quitting her consulting job and the Vice Chairman writing a check for $10,000 and giving her an introduction into an incubator.
The experience of launching ClassPass, getting press and Inc Magazine cover, and then nobody signing up.
contrarian_claims: Society's definition of success (job, marriage, house, kids) can make life feel small and limit dreams.
Entrepreneurship isn't about following a blueprint, but about having no plan and figuring out your mission.
The message of what a billion dollar valuation represents to so many other girls out there is a more important impact than the billion dollars.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Classpass Founder: Quitting My 9-5 Led To A $1 Billion Business: Payal Kadakia
